Title: Integrative omics reveals mechanisms of biosynthesis and regulation of floral scent in Cymbidium tracyanum

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: petal and sepal flowers; petals are primary site of scent release
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: floral scent for pollinator attraction; horticultural fragrance trait; ecological adaptability
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: terpenoid biosynthesis / floral volatile terpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms volatile terpene products from prenyl diphosphate precursors
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: likely geranyl diphosphate (GPP) in planta; not enzymatically assayed here
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: putative cyclization/rearrangement of prenyl diphosphate precursor(s) to volatile monoterpene product(s); exact product not validated because soluble recombinant protein was not obtained
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in petals and sepals of full-blooming flowers; increases during flowering and is ~50-fold higher in petal/sepal than labellum/gynostemium
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in petals and sepals; highly expressed in scented C. tracyanum but rarely detectable in scentless C. lowianum
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: associated with production of floral volatile terpenoids in scented flowers
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA/co-expression; qRT-PCR; subcellular localization; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtTPS1 was identified from the terpenoid-associated black co-expression module as a key floral scent structural gene. 2. CtTPS1 is plastid-localized and shows strong petal/sepal-preferential expression during blooming, matching terpenoid emission. 3. CtAP2/ERF1 directly activates the CtTPS1 promoter, linking it to transcriptional control of floral scent.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant for specific terpene formation; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CtTPS1 native promoter in reporter assay; CaMV 35S for TF effector constructs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate target for engineering/fragrance breeding in Cymbidium, potentially in combination with activating TFs such as CtAP2/ERF1
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: actual catalytic product unresolved; possible metabolic flux competition with other pathways discussed generally for terpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: flower developmental stage; greenhouse growth at 18–24 °C, 50–70% RH, 30% full sunlight
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genome, transcriptome, volatolomics; NCBI SRA PRJNA1145103
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CtTPS1 could not be induced as soluble recombinant protein in E. coli, so in vitro enzyme activity was not determined.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: volatile monoterpenes and sesquiterpenes contributing to floral scent
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: petal and sepal flowers; petals are major scent release site
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pollinator attraction; fragrance trait; ecological adaptation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: sesquiterpene synthase branch with bifunctional in vitro activity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms floral volatile terpenes from prenyl diphosphates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: With GPP: (E)-β-ocimene, α-terpineol, terpinolene; with FPP: β-sesquiphellandrene, α-curcumene, α-farnesene, α-bisabolene, nerolidol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts GPP to multiple monoterpenes and FPP to multiple sesquiterpenes in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in petals and sepals; significantly higher at all blooming stages than bud despite fluctuation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in scented C. tracyanum; rarely detectable in scentless C. lowianum
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes production of multiple floral volatile monoterpenes and sesquiterpenes in C. tracyanum
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro recombinant enzyme assay with GPP/FPP and GC-MS; qRT-PCR; WGCNA/co-expression; dual-luciferase; Y1H; EMSA; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtTPS2 is a key floral scent TPS highly expressed in scented floral organs and during blooming. 2. Recombinant CtTPS2 is bifunctional in vitro, producing several monoterpenes from GPP and several sesquiterpenes from FPP. 3. CtAP2/ERF4 and CtMYB3 directly activate the CtTPS2 promoter, connecting CtTPS2 to floral scent regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant for volatile terpene profile; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CtTPS2 native promoter in reporter assay; CaMV 35S for TF effector constructs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong candidate for fragrance improvement and metabolic engineering of complex terpene bouquets in Cymbidium
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: bifunctionality implies product outcome may depend on substrate pool and localization; possible flux competition with other terpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genome, transcriptome, volatolomics; NCBI SRA PRJNA1145103
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: actual in planta product spectrum may differ from in vitro outputs because of substrate availability and compartmentation.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: volatile monoterpenes and sesquiterpenes contributing to floral scent
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: sesquiterpene synthase branch with bifunctional in vitro activity
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: With GPP: neo-allo-ocimene; with FPP: β-humulene and (Z,E)-farnesol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts GPP to neo-allo-ocimene and FPP to sesquiterpene/alcohol products in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in petals and sepals and increases during flowering
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in scented C. tracyanum; rarely detectable in scentless C. lowianum
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes biosynthesis of floral scent volatiles including neo-allo-ocimene and sesquiterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro recombinant enzyme assay with GC-MS; qRT-PCR; WGCNA/co-expression; dual-luciferase; Y1H; EMSA; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtTPS3 is a key petal/sepal-expressed TPS associated with floral terpenoid emission. 2. Recombinant CtTPS3 is bifunctional in vitro, making neo-allo-ocimene from GPP and β-humulene/(Z,E)-farnesol from FPP. 3. CtMYB2 and CtMYB3 directly activate the CtTPS3 promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant for specific volatile products; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CtTPS3 native promoter in reporter assay; CaMV 35S for TF effector constructs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful target for tailoring floral monoterpene/sesquiterpene notes in orchid breeding
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: in planta products may depend on substrate availability and localization
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genome, transcriptome, volatolomics; NCBI SRA PRJNA1145103

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: petal and sepal flowers; petals are major scent release site
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major contributor to floral scent; pollinator attraction; horticultural fragrance trait
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms major floral scent volatile(s) from prenyl diphosphates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: With GPP: neo-allo-ocimene and terpinolene; with FPP: mainly (E)-β-caryophyllene
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts GPP to monoterpenes and FPP primarily to (E)-β-caryophyllene in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in petals and sepals; significantly higher at all blooming stages than bud despite fluctuations
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in scented C. tracyanum; rarely detectable in scentless C. lowianum
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes biosynthesis of major floral scent sesquiterpene (E)-β-caryophyllene and other volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro recombinant enzyme assay with GPP/FPP and GC-MS; qRT-PCR; WGCNA/co-expression; dual-luciferase; Y1H; EMSA; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtTPS8 is a major floral scent TPS whose expression pattern matches terpene emission during flower opening. 2. Recombinant CtTPS8 is bifunctional and produces neo-allo-ocimene/terpinolene from GPP and mainly (E)-β-caryophyllene from FPP. 3. CtTPS8 is a regulatory hub activated by multiple TFs including CtAP2/ERF1, CtAP2/ERF4, CtbZIP1, CtMYB2 and CtMYB3.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: major terminal determinant for (E)-β-caryophyllene accumulation; not explicitly called rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CtTPS8 native promoter in reporter assay; CaMV 35S for TF effector constructs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: high-value engineering target for increasing major floral scent volatiles and fragrant Cymbidium breeding
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: product profile depends on substrate availability/localization; increasing volatile flux may compete with other terpene uses
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genome, transcriptome, volatolomics; NCBI SRA PRJNA1145103
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: (E)-β-caryophyllene was the most abundant terpenoid during flowering.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional activation of floral volatile terpenoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation by direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expression peaks around D+7 and is higher in scented C. tracyanum than in scentless C. lowianum; expressed in petal, sepal and gynostemium
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: developmentally regulated during flowering, preceding structural gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes floral scent terpenoid production by activating key CtTPS genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA/co-expression; qRT-PCR; nuclear localization; dual-luciferase reporter assay; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtAP2/ERF1 is a candidate hub TF co-expressed with key CtTPS genes. 2. It directly binds the CtTPS1 promoter AP2 element and CtTPS8 promoter DRE element. 3. It activates CtTPS1 and CtTPS8 promoters, supporting a positive role in floral scent terpenoid biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: direct activator of two key TPS genes linked to floral scent output
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promising regulator for boosting fragrance, especially in combination with CtTPS1/CtTPS8
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S for effector constructs; native CtTPS1/CtTPS8 promoters in reporters
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: one of the six TFs prioritized as key regulator.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional activation of terpene synthase genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation by direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: peaks around D+7; highly expressed in petal and sepal; higher in scented C. tracyanum than in scentless C. lowianum
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: developmentally regulated and precedes structural gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes floral scent terpenoid production by activating CtTPS2 and CtTPS8
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA/co-expression; qRT-PCR; nuclear localization; dual-luciferase; Y1H; EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtAP2/ERF4 is a high-confidence floral scent regulator in C. tracyanum. 2. It directly binds the AP2 element in the CtTPS2 promoter and DRE element in the CtTPS8 promoter. 3. It activates CtTPS2 and CtTPS8, linking AP2/ERF signaling to monoterpene/sesquiterpene biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: direct activator of two key TPS genes, including CtTPS8 for major β-caryophyllene production
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful transcriptional regulator for fragrance enhancement
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S for effector constructs; native CtTPS2/CtTPS8 promoters in reporters
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: identified as one of the key structural gene regulators with petal/sepal-enriched expression.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional activation of terpene synthase expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation by direct promoter binding to G-box/ABRE motifs
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: flower developmental stage; possible ABA-related cis context inferred from ABRE in target promoter
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: peaks around D+15; highly expressed in petal and sepal; higher in scented C. tracyanum than in scentless C. lowianum
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: developmentally regulated and co-expressed with key TPS genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes production of floral scent terpenoids through activation of CtTPS8
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA/co-expression; qRT-PCR; nuclear localization; dual-luciferase; Y1H; EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtbZIP1 is a hub co-expression TF associated with floral terpenoid biosynthesis. 2. It directly binds G-box and ABRE cis-elements in the CtTPS8 promoter. 3. It activates CtTPS8 expression, supporting a positive role in formation of major floral scent terpenes.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: direct regulator of CtTPS8, a major enzyme for β-caryophyllene-rich scent output
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate upstream regulator for engineering stronger fragrance
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S for effector constructs; native CtTPS8 promoter in reporter
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: among TFs highly co-expressed with CtTPS1/2/3/8 in the black module.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional activation of terpene synthase genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation by direct promoter binding to MYB sites
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in petal and sepal; higher in scented C. tracyanum than in scentless C. lowianum; developmentally regulated
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: peaks during blooming and precedes structural gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enhances floral scent terpenoid biosynthesis by activating CtTPS3 and CtTPS8
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA/co-expression; qRT-PCR; nuclear localization; dual-luciferase; Y1H; EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtMYB2 is a candidate hub regulator positively associated with floral terpenoid accumulation. 2. It directly binds MYB sites, including multiple MYB sites in the CtTPS3 promoter, and activates CtTPS3 and CtTPS8. 3. Its expression pattern in scented tissues and stages supports a positive role in floral scent regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: direct activator of two key TPS genes that generate major monoterpene/sesquiterpene volatiles
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: valuable regulator for coordinated upregulation of multiple fragrance enzymes
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S for effector constructs; native CtTPS3/CtTPS8 promoters in reporters
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: the text states CtMYB2 specifically interacted with three MYB sites in the CtTPS3 promoter.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional activation of terpene synthase genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation by direct promoter binding to MYB sites
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates CtTPS2, CtTPS3 and CtTPS8 promoters
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in petal and sepal; higher in scented C. tracyanum than in scentless C. lowianum; developmentally regulated
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: peaks during blooming and precedes structural gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: broad positive regulator of floral volatile terpenoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA/co-expression; qRT-PCR; nuclear localization; dual-luciferase; Y1H; EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtMYB3 directly activates three key TPS genes: CtTPS2, CtTPS3 and CtTPS8. 2. It is highly expressed in scented tissues and developmental stages consistent with terpene emission. 3. The authors propose CtMYB3 is likely a key transcription factor regulating floral scent volatiles in C. tracyanum.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: likely key transcription factor because it activates multiple major TPS genes simultaneously
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: excellent candidate master-like regulator for fragrance breeding/metabolic engineering
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S for effector constructs; native CtTPS2/CtTPS3/CtTPS8 promoters in reporters
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: discussion specifically highlights CtMYB3 as likely key TF requiring further study.
